---
title: "It's about time"
author: "Seongwon Cho"
execute: 
  warning: false
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    code-line-numbers: true
project:
  type: website
  output-dir: docs
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(riem)
library(dplyr)
```

```{r}
data <- read.csv("https://byuistats.github.io/M335/data/sales.csv")
#view(data)
```

```{r}
data1 <- data %>%
mutate(
datetime_utc = ymd_hms(Time),
datetime_mt = with_tz(datetime_utc, tzone = "America/Boise"),
day = as_date(datetime_mt),
week = floor_date(datetime_mt, "week"),
month = floor_date(datetime_mt, "month")
)
#view(data1)
```

```{r}
revenue_daily <- data1 %>%
  group_by(Name, day) %>%
  summarise(total_revenue = sum(Amount, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  rename(date = day)

revenue_weekly <- data1 %>%
  group_by(Name, week) %>%
  summarise(total_revenue = sum(Amount, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  rename(date = week)

revenue_monthly <- data1 %>%
  group_by(Name, month) %>%
  summarise(total_revenue = sum(Amount, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  rename(date = month)

revenue_agg <- bind_rows(
  "Daily" = revenue_daily,
  "Weekly" = revenue_weekly,
  "Monthly" = revenue_monthly,
  .id = "Aggregation"
) %>%
  mutate(
    Aggregation = factor(Aggregation, levels = c("Daily", "Weekly", "Monthly"))
  )

#view(revenue_agg)
```

From this visualization, I think HotDiggity performed the best. It has the highest revenue generally. LeBelle has the highest revenue value in a week plot, but it also has extreme up and down. Thus, HotDiggity has the best revenue.

```{r, fig.width=12}
ggplot(revenue_agg, aes(x = date, y = total_revenue, color = Name, group = Name)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.8, linewidth = 1.1) +
  # Add points to weekly and monthly plots
  geom_point(data = filter(revenue_agg, Aggregation != "Daily"), size = 2) +
  
  # Facet by the aggregation level
  facet_wrap(~ Aggregation, scales = "free", ncol = 3) +
  
  # Format labels
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b %d") +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  
  # Add titles
  labs(
    title = "Gross Revenue Over Time by Company",
    subtitle = "Data aggregated at Daily, Weekly, and Monthly levels",
    x = "Date",
    y = "Gross Revenue",
    color = "Company"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```
